4	
5	import _root_.logger.{LogLevel, Logger}
6	import chisel3.BuildInfo.{firtoolVersion, version => chiselVersion}
7	import chisel3.InternalErrorException
8	import chisel3.experimental.hierarchy.core.ImportDefinitionAnnotation
9	import chisel3.stage.{ChiselCircuitAnnotation, ChiselOptions, ChiselOptionsView, DesignAnnotation, SourceRootAnnotation}
10	import circt.stage.{CIRCTOptions, CIRCTOptionsView, CIRCTTarget, EmittedMLIR, PreserveAggregate}
11	import firrtl.annotations.JsonProtocol
12	import firrtl.ir.CircuitWithAnnos
13	import firrtl.options.Viewer.view
14	import firrtl.options.{
15	  CustomFileEmission,
16	  Dependency,
17	  OptionsException,
18	  Phase,
19	  StageOptions,
20	  StageOptionsView,
21	  Unserializable
22	}
23	import firrtl.options.StageUtils.dramaticMessage
24	import firrtl.stage.{FirrtlOptions, FirrtlOptionsView}
25	import firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, seqToAnnoSeq, AnnotationSeq, EmittedVerilogCircuit, EmittedVerilogCircuitAnnotation}
26	
27	import java.io.File
28	import scala.collection.mutable
29	import scala.util.control.NoStackTrace
30	import firrtl.EmittedBtor2CircuitAnnotation
31	import firrtl.EmittedBtor2Circuit
32	import org.json4s.convertToJsonInput
33	
34	private object Helpers {
35	  implicit class LogLevelHelpers(logLevel: LogLevel.Value) {
36	    def toCIRCTOptions: Seq[String] = logLevel match {
37	      case LogLevel.Error => Seq.empty
38	      case LogLevel.Warn  => Seq.empty
39	      case LogLevel.Info  => Seq("-verbose-pass-executions")
40	      case LogLevel.Debug => Seq("-verbose-pass-executions")
41	      case LogLevel.Trace => Seq("-verbose-pass-executions", "-mlir-print-ir-after-all")
42	      case LogLevel.None  => Seq.empty
43	    }
44	  }
45	
46	  /** Extract the JSON-encoded Annotation region from a single file output.
47	    *
48	    * @todo This is very janky and should be changed to something more stable.
49	    */
50	  def extractAnnotationFile(string: String, filename: String): AnnotationSeq = {
51	    var inAnno = false
52	    val filtered: String = string.linesIterator.filter {
53	      case line if line.startsWith("// ----- 8< ----- FILE") && line.contains(filename) =>
54	        inAnno = true
55	        false
56	      case line if line.startsWith("// ----- 8< ----- FILE") =>
57	        inAnno = false
58	        false
59	      case line if inAnno =>
60	        true
61	
62	      case _ => false
63	    }.toArray
64	      .mkString("\n")
65	    filtered.forall(_.isWhitespace) match {
66	      case false => JsonProtocol.deserialize(filtered, false)
67	      case true  => Seq.empty
68	    }
69	  }
70	
71	  class LoggerShim(logger: Logger) extends firtoolresolver.Logger {
72	    def error(msg: String): Unit = logger.error(msg)
73	    def warn(msg:  String): Unit = logger.warn(msg)
74	    def info(msg:  String): Unit = logger.info(msg)
75	    def debug(msg: String): Unit = logger.debug(msg)
76	    def trace(msg: String): Unit = logger.trace(msg)
77	  }
78	}
79	
80	private[this] object Exceptions {
81	
82	  def versionAdvice: String =
83	    s"Note that this version of Chisel ($chiselVersion) was published against firtool version " +
84	      firtoolVersion.getOrElse("<unknown>") + "."
85	
86	  /** Indicates that the firtool binary failed with a non-zero exit code.  This generally indicates a compiler error
87	    * either originating from a user error or from a crash.
88	    *
89	    * @param binary the path to the firtool binary
90	    * @param exitCode the numeric exit status code returned by the firtool binary
91	    * @param stdout the contents returned to standard out by the firtool binary
92	    * @param stderr the contents returned to standard error by the firtool binary
93	    */
94	  class FirtoolNonZeroExitCode(binary: String, exitCode: Int, stdout: String, stderr: String)
95	      extends RuntimeException(
96	        dramaticMessage(
97	          header = Some(s"${binary} returned a non-zero exit code. $versionAdvice"),
98	          body = s"ExitCode:\n${exitCode}\nSTDOUT:\n${stdout}\nSTDERR:\n${stderr}"
99	        )
100	      )
101	      with NoStackTrace
102	
103	  /** Indicates that the firtool binary was not found. This likely indicates something is wrong with
104	    *  their firtool installation.
105	    *
106	    * @param msg the error message
107	    */
108	  class FirtoolNotFound(msg: String)
109	      extends RuntimeException(
110	        dramaticMessage(
111	          header = Some(s"Error resolving firtool"),
112	          body = s"""|Chisel requires firtool, the MLIR-based FIRRTL Compiler (MFC), to generate Verilog.
113	                     |Something is wrong with your firtool installation, please see the following logging
114	                     |information.
115	                     |$msg""".stripMargin
116	        )
117	      )
118	      with NoStackTrace
119	
120	}
121	
122	/** A phase that calls and runs CIRCT, specifically `firtool`, while preserving an [[firrtl.AnnotationSeq AnnotationSeq]] API. */
123	// TODO this uses the converted FIRRTL circuit yet doesn't depend on anything--probably needs fixing and maybe we can stop using the FIRRTL CIRCT
124	class CIRCT extends Phase {
125	
126	  import Helpers._
127	
128	  import scala.sys.process._
129	
130	  override def prerequisites = Seq(
131	    Dependency[circt.stage.phases.AddImplicitOutputFile]
132	  )
133	  override def optionalPrerequisites = Seq.empty
134	  override def optionalPrerequisiteOf = Seq.empty
135	  override def invalidates(a: Phase) = false
136	
137	  override def transform(annotations: AnnotationSeq): AnnotationSeq = {
138	    val circtOptions = view[CIRCTOptions](annotations)
139	
140	    // Early exit (do not run firtool) if the target is "CHIRRTL", i.e., specification FIRRTL.
141	    circtOptions.target match {
142	      case Some(CIRCTTarget.CHIRRTL) => return annotations
143	      case _                         =>
144	    }
145	
146	    val stageOptions = view[StageOptions](annotations)
147	    val firrtlOptions = view[FirrtlOptions](annotations)
148	    val chiselOptions = view[ChiselOptions](annotations)
149	
150	    var logLevel = _root_.logger.LogLevel.None
151	    var split = circtOptions.splitVerilog
152	    val includeDirs = mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[String]
153	
154	    // Partition the annotations into those that will be passed to CIRCT and
155	    // those that are not.  The annotations that are in the passhtrough set will
156	    // be returned without modification.
157	    val (passthroughAnnotations, circtAnnotations) = annotations.partition {
158	      case _: ImportDefinitionAnnotation[_] | _: DesignAnnotation[_] | _: ChiselCircuitAnnotation =>
159	        true
160	      case _ => false
161	    }
162	
163	    val annotationsx: AnnotationSeq = circtAnnotations.flatMap {
164	      case a: CustomFileEmission => {
165	        val filename = a.filename(annotations)
166	        a.replacements(filename)
167	      }
168	      case _:    ImportDefinitionAnnotation[_] => Nil
169	      case anno: _root_.logger.LogLevelAnnotation =>
170	        logLevel = anno.globalLogLevel
171	        Nil
172	      case SourceRootAnnotation(dir) =>
173	        includeDirs += dir.toString
174	        Nil
175	      /* The following can be dropped. */
176	      case _: _root_.logger.ClassLogLevelAnnotation => Nil
177	      /* Default case: leave the annotation around and let firtool warn about it. */
178	      case a => Seq(a)
179	    }
180	
181	    /* Filter the annotations to only those things which CIRCT should see. */
182	    val filteredAnnotations = annotationsx.flatMap {
183	      case _: ChiselCircuitAnnotation => None
184	      case _: Unserializable          => None
185	      case _: CustomFileEmission      => None
186	      case a => Some(a)
187	    }
188	
189	    val (serialization: Iterable[String], circuitName: String) = chiselOptions.elaboratedCircuit match {
190	      case None => throw new OptionsException("No input file specified!")
191	      // TODO can we avoid converting, how else would we include filteredAnnos?
192	      case Some(circuit) =>
193	        (circuit.lazilySerialize(filteredAnnotations), circuit.name)
194	    }
195	
196	    // FIRRTL is serialized either in memory or to a file
197	    val input: Either[Iterable[String], os.Path] =
198	      if (circtOptions.dumpFir) {
199	        val td = os.Path(stageOptions.targetDir, os.pwd)
200	        val filename = firrtlOptions.outputFileName.getOrElse(circuitName)
201	        val firPath = td / s"$filename.fir"
202	        os.write.over(firPath, serialization, createFolders = true)
203	        Right(firPath)
204	      } else {
205	        Left(serialization)
206	      }
207	
208	    val chiselAnnotationFilename: Option[String] =
209	      stageOptions.annotationFileOut.map(stageOptions.getBuildFileName(_, Some(".anno.json")))
210	
211	    val circtAnnotationFilename = "circt.anno.json"
212	
213	    val binary = circtOptions.firtoolBinaryPath.getOrElse {
214	      // .get is safe, firtoolVersion is an Option for backwards compatibility
215	      val version = firtoolVersion.get
216	      val resolved = firtoolresolver.Resolve(new LoggerShim(logger), version)
217	      resolved match {
218	        case Left(msg) =>
219	          throw new Exceptions.FirtoolNotFound(msg)
220	        case Right(bin) =>
221	          bin.path.toString
222	      }
223	    }
224	
225	    val cmd = // Only 1 of input or firFile will be Some
226	      Seq(binary, input.fold(_ => "-format=fir", _.toString)) ++
227	        Seq("-warn-on-unprocessed-annotations") ++
228	        Seq("-output-annotation-file", circtAnnotationFilename) ++
229	        circtOptions.firtoolOptions ++
230	        logLevel.toCIRCTOptions ++
231	        /* The following options are on by default, so we disable them if they are false. */
232	        (circtOptions.preserveAggregate match {
233	          case Some(PreserveAggregate.OneDimVec) => Seq("-preserve-aggregate=1d-vec")
234	          case Some(PreserveAggregate.Vec)       => Seq("-preserve-aggregate=vec")
235	          case Some(PreserveAggregate.All)       => Seq("-preserve-aggregate=all")
236	          case None                              => None
237	        }) ++
238	        circtOptions.preserveAggregate.map(_ => "-scalarize-public-modules=0") ++
239	        includeDirs.flatMap(d => Seq("--include-dir", d.toString)) ++
240	        /* Convert the target to a firtool-compatible option. */
241	        ((circtOptions.target, split) match {
242	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.FIRRTL), false)        => Seq("-ir-fir")
243	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.HW), false)            => Seq("-ir-hw")
244	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.Verilog), true)        => Seq("--split-verilog", s"-o=${stageOptions.targetDir}")
245	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.Verilog), false)       => None
246	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.SystemVerilog), true)  => Seq("--split-verilog", s"-o=${stageOptions.targetDir}")
247	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.SystemVerilog), false) => None
248	          case (Some(CIRCTTarget.Btor2), false)         => Seq("--btor2")
249	          case (None, _) =>
250	            throw new Exception(
251	              "No 'circtOptions.target' specified. This should be impossible if dependencies are satisfied!"
252	            )
253	          case (_, true) =>
254	            throw new Exception(
255	              s"The circtOptions.target specified (${circtOptions.target}) does not support running with an EmitAllModulesAnnotation as CIRCT only supports one-file-per-module for Verilog or SystemVerilog targets."
256	            )
257	          case _ =>
258	            throw new Exception(
259	              s"Invalid combination of circtOptions.target ${circtOptions.target} and split ${split}"
260	            )
261	        })
262	
263	    logger.info(s"""Running CIRCT: '${cmd.mkString(" ")}""" + input.fold(_ => " < $$" + "input'", _ => "'"))
264	    val stdoutStream, stderrStream = new java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream
265	    val stdoutWriter = new java.io.PrintWriter(stdoutStream)
266	    val stderrWriter = new java.io.PrintWriter(stderrStream)
267	    val stdin: os.ProcessInput = input match {
268	      case Left(it) => (it: os.Source) // Static cast to apply implicit conversion
269	      case Right(_) => os.Pipe
270	    }
271	    val stdout = os.ProcessOutput.Readlines(stdoutWriter.println)
272	    val stderr = os.ProcessOutput.Readlines(stderrWriter.println)
273	    val exitValue =
274	      try {
275	        os.proc(cmd).call(check = false, stdin = stdin, stdout = stdout, stderr = stderr).exitCode
276	      } catch {
277	        case a: java.io.IOException if a.getMessage().startsWith("Cannot run program") =>
278	          throw new Exceptions.FirtoolNotFound(a.getMessage())
279	      }
280	    stdoutWriter.close()
281	    stderrWriter.close()
282	    val result = stdoutStream.toString
283	    logger.info(result)
284	    val errors = stderrStream.toString
285	    if (exitValue != 0)
286	      throw new Exceptions.FirtoolNonZeroExitCode(binary, exitValue, result, errors)
287	    val finalAnnotations = if (split) {
288	      logger.info(result)
289	      val file = new File(stageOptions.getBuildFileName(circtAnnotationFilename, Some(".anno.json")))
290	      file match {
291	        case file if !file.canRead() => Seq.empty
292	        case file => {
293	          val foo = JsonProtocol.deserialize(file, false)
294	          foo
295	        }
296	      }
297	    } else { // if split it has already been written out to the file system stdout not necessary.
298	      val outputFileName: String = stageOptions.getBuildFileName(firrtlOptions.outputFileName.get)
299	      val outputAnnotations = extractAnnotationFile(result, circtAnnotationFilename)
300	      outputAnnotations ++ (circtOptions.target match {
301	        case Some(CIRCTTarget.FIRRTL) =>
302	          Seq(EmittedMLIR(outputFileName, result, Some(".fir.mlir")))
303	        case Some(CIRCTTarget.HW) =>
304	          Seq(EmittedMLIR(outputFileName, result, Some(".hw.mlir")))
305	        case Some(CIRCTTarget.Verilog) =>
306	          Seq(EmittedVerilogCircuitAnnotation(EmittedVerilogCircuit(outputFileName, result, ".v")))
307	        case Some(CIRCTTarget.Btor2) =>
308	          Seq(EmittedBtor2CircuitAnnotation(EmittedBtor2Circuit(outputFileName, result, ".btor2")))
309	        case Some(CIRCTTarget.SystemVerilog) =>
310	          Seq(EmittedVerilogCircuitAnnotation(EmittedVerilogCircuit(outputFileName, result, ".sv")))
311	        case None =>
312	          throw new Exception(
313	            "No 'circtOptions.target' specified. This should be impossible if dependencies are satisfied!"
314	          )
315	        case unknown =>
316	          throw new InternalErrorException(s"Match Error: Unknon CIRCTTarget: $unknown")
317	      })
318	    }
319	
320	    // Return the passthrough annotations and the output annotations from CIRCT.
321	    passthroughAnnotations ++ finalAnnotations
322	  }
323	
324	}

content.content:
1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package firrtl.options
4	
5	import firrtl.AnnotationSeq
6	import firrtl.graph.{CyclicException, DiGraph}
7	
8	import scala.collection.Set
9	import scala.collection.immutable.{Set => ISet}
10	import scala.collection.mutable.{ArrayBuffer, HashMap, LinkedHashMap, LinkedHashSet, Queue}
11	
12	/** An exception arising from an in a [[DependencyManager]] */
13	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
14	case class DependencyManagerException(message: String, cause: Throwable = null) extends RuntimeException(message, cause)
15	
16	/** A [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] that resolves a linear ordering of dependencies based on
17	  * requirements.
18	  * @tparam A the type over which this transforms
19	  * @tparam B the type of the [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]
20	  */
21	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
22	trait DependencyManager[A, B <: TransformLike[A] with DependencyAPI[B]] extends TransformLike[A] with DependencyAPI[B] {
23	  import DependencyManagerUtils.CharSet
24	
25	  override def prerequisites: Seq[Dependency[B]] = currentState
26	
27	  override def optionalPrerequisites: Seq[Dependency[B]] = Seq.empty
28	
29	  override def optionalPrerequisiteOf: Seq[Dependency[B]] = Seq.empty
30	
31	  override def invalidates(a: B): Boolean = (_currentState &~ _targets)(oToD(a))
32	
33	  /** Requested [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]s that should be run. Internally, this will be converted to
34	    * a set based on the ordering defined here.
35	    */
36	  def targets: Seq[Dependency[B]]
37	  private lazy val _targets: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]] = targets
38	    .foldLeft(new LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]]()) { case (a, b) => a += b }
39	
40	  /** A sequence of [[TransformLike]]s that have been run. Internally, this will be converted to an ordered set.
41	    */
42	  def currentState: Seq[Dependency[B]]
43	  private lazy val _currentState: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]] = currentState
44	    .foldLeft(new LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]]()) { case (a, b) => a += b }
45	
46	  /** Existing transform objects that have already been constructed */
47	  def knownObjects: Set[B]
48	
49	  /** A sequence of wrappers to apply to the resulting [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] sequence. This can
50	    * be used to, e.g., add automated pre-processing and post-processing.
51	    */
52	  def wrappers: Seq[(B) => B] = Seq.empty
53	
54	  /** Store of conversions between classes and objects. Objects that do not exist in the map will be lazily constructed.
55	    */
56	  protected lazy val dependencyToObject: LinkedHashMap[Dependency[B], B] = {
57	    val init = LinkedHashMap[Dependency[B], B](knownObjects.map(x => oToD(x) -> x).toSeq: _*)
58	    (_targets ++ _currentState)
59	      .filter(!init.contains(_))
60	      .map(x => init(x) = x.getObject())
61	    init
62	  }
63	
64	  /** A method that will create a copy of this [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager DependencyManager]], but with different
65	    * requirements. This is used to solve sub-problems arising from invalidations.
66	    */
67	  protected def copy(
68	    targets:      Seq[Dependency[B]],
69	    currentState: Seq[Dependency[B]],
70	    knownObjects: ISet[B] = dependencyToObject.values.toSet
71	  ): B
72	
73	  /** Implicit conversion from Dependency to B */
74	  private implicit def dToO(d: Dependency[B]): B = dependencyToObject.getOrElseUpdate(d, d.getObject())
75	
76	  /** Implicit conversion from B to Dependency */
77	  private implicit def oToD(b: B): Dependency[B] = Dependency.fromTransform(b)
78	
79	  /** Modified breadth-first search that supports multiple starting nodes and a custom extractor that can be used to
80	    * generate/filter the edges to explore. Additionally, this will include edges to previously discovered nodes.
81	    */
82	  private def bfs(
83	    start:     LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]],
84	    blacklist: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]],
85	    extractor: B => Set[Dependency[B]]
86	  ): LinkedHashMap[B, LinkedHashSet[B]] = {
87	
88	    val (queue, edges) = {
89	      val a: Queue[Dependency[B]] = Queue(start.toSeq: _*)
90	      val b: LinkedHashMap[B, LinkedHashSet[B]] =
91	        LinkedHashMap[B, LinkedHashSet[B]](start.map((dToO(_) -> LinkedHashSet[B]())).toSeq: _*)
92	      (a, b)
93	    }
94	
95	    while (queue.nonEmpty) {
96	      val u: Dependency[B] = queue.dequeue()
97	      for (v <- extractor(dependencyToObject(u))) {
98	        if (!blacklist.contains(v) && !edges.contains(v)) {
99	          queue.enqueue(v)
100	        }
101	        if (!edges.contains(v)) {
102	          val obj = dToO(v)
103	          edges(obj) = LinkedHashSet.empty
104	          dependencyToObject += (v -> obj)
105	        }
106	        edges(dependencyToObject(u)) += dependencyToObject(v)
107	      }
108	    }
109	
110	    edges
111	  }
112	
113	  /** Pull in all registered [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] once [[firrtl.options.TransformLike
114	    * TransformLike]] registration is integrated
115	    * @todo implement this
116	    */
117	  private lazy val registeredTransforms: Set[B] = Set.empty
118	
119	  /** A directed graph consisting of prerequisite edges */
120	  private lazy val prerequisiteGraph: DiGraph[B] = {
121	    val edges = bfs(
122	      start = _targets &~ _currentState,
123	      blacklist = _currentState,
124	      extractor = (p: B) => p._prerequisites &~ _currentState
125	    )
126	    DiGraph(edges)
127	  }
128	
129	  /** A directed graph consisting of prerequisites derived from only those transforms which are supposed to run. This
130	    * pulls in optionalPrerequisiteOf for transforms which are not in the target set.
131	    */
132	  private lazy val optionalPrerequisiteOfGraph: DiGraph[B] = {
133	    val v = new LinkedHashSet() ++ prerequisiteGraph.getVertices
134	    DiGraph(new LinkedHashMap() ++ v.map(vv => vv -> (v & (vv._optionalPrerequisiteOf.toSet).map(dToO)))).reverse
135	  }
136	
137	  /** A directed graph of *optional* prerequisites. Each optional prerequisite is promoted to a full prerequisite if the
138	    * optional prerequisite is already a node in the prerequisite graph.
139	    */
140	  private lazy val optionalPrerequisitesGraph: DiGraph[B] = {
141	    val v = new LinkedHashSet() ++ prerequisiteGraph.getVertices
142	    DiGraph(new LinkedHashMap() ++ v.map(vv => vv -> (v & (vv._optionalPrerequisites).map(dToO))))
143	  }
144	
145	  /** A directed graph consisting of prerequisites derived from ALL targets. This is necessary for defining targets for
146	    * [[DependencyManager]] sub-problems.
147	    */
148	  private lazy val otherPrerequisites: DiGraph[B] = {
149	    val edges = {
150	      val x = new LinkedHashMap ++ _targets
151	        .map(dependencyToObject)
152	        .map { a => a -> prerequisiteGraph.getVertices.filter(a._optionalPrerequisiteOf(_)) }
153	      x.values
154	        .reduce(_ ++ _)
155	        .foldLeft(x) { case (xx, y) =>
156	          if (xx.contains(y)) { xx }
157	          else { xx ++ Map(y -> Set.empty[B]) }
158	        }
159	    }
160	    DiGraph(edges).reverse
161	  }
162	
163	  /** A directed graph consisting of all prerequisites, including prerequisites derived from optionalPrerequisites and
164	    * optionalPrerequisiteOf
165	    */
166	  lazy val dependencyGraph: DiGraph[B] = prerequisiteGraph + optionalPrerequisiteOfGraph + optionalPrerequisitesGraph
167	
168	  /** A directed graph consisting of invalidation edges */
169	  lazy val invalidateGraph: DiGraph[B] = {
170	    val v = new LinkedHashSet() ++ dependencyGraph.getVertices
171	    DiGraph(
172	      bfs(
173	        start = v.map(oToD(_)),
174	        blacklist = _currentState,
175	        /* Explore all invalidated transforms **EXCEPT** the current transform! */
176	        extractor = (p: B) => {
177	          val filtered = new LinkedHashSet[Dependency[B]]
178	          filtered ++= v.filter(p.invalidates).map(oToD(_))
179	          filtered -= oToD(p)
180	          filtered
181	        }
182	      )
183	    ).reverse
184	  }
185	
186	  /** Wrap a possible [[CyclicException]] thrown by a thunk in a [[DependencyManagerException]] */
187	  private def cyclePossible[A](a: String, diGraph: DiGraph[_])(thunk: => A): A = try { thunk }
188	  catch {
189	    case e: CyclicException =>
190	      throw new DependencyManagerException(
191	        s"""|No transform ordering possible due to cyclic dependency in $a with cycles:
192	            |${diGraph.findSCCs.filter(_.size > 1).mkString("    - ", "\n    - ", "")}""".stripMargin,
193	        e
194	      )
195	  }
196	
197	  /** An ordering of [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]s that causes the requested [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager.targets
198	    * targets]] to be executed starting from the [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager.currentState currentState]]. This ordering respects
199	    * prerequisites, optionalPrerequisites, optionalPrerequisiteOf, and invalidates of all constituent
200	    * [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]s. This uses an algorithm that attempts to reduce the number of
201	    * re-lowerings due to invalidations. Re-lowerings are implemented as new [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager]]s.
202	    * @throws firrtl.options.DependencyManagerException if a cycle exists in either the [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager.dependencyGraph
203	    * dependencyGraph]] or the [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager.invalidateGraph invalidateGraph]].
204	    */
205	  lazy val transformOrder: Seq[B] = {
206	
207	    /* Topologically sort the dependency graph to determine a "good" initial ordering.
208	     */
209	    val sorted = {
210	      val edges = {
211	        val v = cyclePossible("invalidates", invalidateGraph) { invalidateGraph.linearize }.reverse
212	        /* A comparison function that will sort vertices based on the topological sort of the invalidation graph */
213	        val cmp =
214	          (l: B, r: B) =>
215	            v.foldLeft((Map.empty[B, Dependency[B] => Boolean], ISet.empty[Dependency[B]])) { case ((m, s), r) =>
216	              (m + (r -> ((a: Dependency[B]) => !s(a))), s + r)
217	            }._1(l)(r)
218	        new LinkedHashMap() ++
219	          v.map(vv => vv -> (new LinkedHashSet() ++ (dependencyGraph.getEdges(vv).toSeq.sortWith(cmp))))
220	      }
221	
222	      cyclePossible("prerequisites", dependencyGraph) {
223	        DiGraph(edges).linearize.reverse
224	          .dropWhile(b => _currentState.contains(b))
225	      }
226	    }
227	
228	    /* [todo] Seq is inefficient here, but Array has ClassTag problems. Use something else? */
229	    val (s, l) = sorted.foldLeft((_currentState, Seq[B]())) { case ((state, out), in) =>
230	      val prereqs = in._prerequisites ++
231	        dependencyGraph.getEdges(in).toSeq.map(oToD) ++
232	        otherPrerequisites.getEdges(in).toSeq.map(oToD)
233	      val preprocessing: Option[B] = {
234	        if ((prereqs.diff(state)).nonEmpty) { Some(this.copy(prereqs.toSeq, state.toSeq)) }
235	        else { None }
236	      }
237	      /* "in" is added *after* invalidation because a transform my not invalidate itself! */
238	      ((state ++ prereqs).map(dToO).filterNot(in.invalidates).map(oToD) += in, out ++ preprocessing :+ in)
239	    }
240	    val postprocessing: Option[B] = {
241	      if ((_targets.diff(s)).nonEmpty) { Some(this.copy(_targets.toSeq, s.toSeq)) }
242	      else { None }
243	    }
244	    l ++ postprocessing
245	  }
246	
247	  /** A version of the [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager.transformOrder transformOrder]] that flattens the transforms of any internal
248	    * [[firrtl.options.DependencyManager DependencyManager]]s.
249	    */
250	  lazy val flattenedTransformOrder: Seq[B] = transformOrder.flatMap {
251	    case p: DependencyManager[A, B] => p.flattenedTransformOrder
252	    case p => Some(p)
253	  }
254	
255	  final override def transform(annotations: A): A = {
256	
257	    /* A local store of each wrapper to it's underlying class. */
258	    val wrapperToClass = new HashMap[B, Dependency[B]]
259	
260	    /* The determined, flat order of transforms is wrapped with surrounding transforms while populating wrapperToClass so
261	     * that each wrapped transform object can be dereferenced to its underlying class. Each wrapped transform is then
262	     * applied while tracking the state of the underlying A. If the state ever disagrees with a prerequisite, then this
263	     * throws an exception.
264	     */
265	    flattenedTransformOrder.map { t =>
266	      val w = wrappers.foldLeft(t) { case (tx, wrapper) => wrapper(tx) }
267	      wrapperToClass += (w -> t)
268	      w
269	    }.foldLeft((annotations, _currentState)) { case ((a, state), t) =>
270	      if (!t.prerequisites.toSet.subsetOf(state)) {
271	        throw new DependencyManagerException(
272	          s"""|Tried to execute '$t' for which run-time prerequisites were not satisfied:
273	              |  state: ${state.mkString("\n    -", "\n    -", "")}
274	              |  prerequisites: ${prerequisites.mkString("\n    -", "\n    -", "")}""".stripMargin
275	        )
276	      }
277	      val logger = t.getLogger
278	      logger.info(s"======== Starting ${t.name} ========")
279	      val (timeMillis, annosx) = firrtl.Utils.time { t.transform(a) }
280	      logger.info(s"""----------------------------${"-" * t.name.size}---------\n""")
281	      logger.info(f"Time: $timeMillis%.1f ms")
282	      logger.info(s"======== Finished ${t.name} ========")
283	      val statex = (state += wrapperToClass(t)).map(dToO).filterNot(t.invalidates).map(oToD)
284	      (annosx, statex)
285	    }._1
286	  }
287	
288	  /** This colormap uses Colorbrewer's 4-class OrRd color scheme */
289	  protected val colormap = Seq("#fef0d9", "#fdcc8a", "#fc8d59", "#d7301f")
290	
291	  /** Get a name of some [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] */
292	  private def transformName(transform: B, suffix: String = ""): String = s""""${transform.name}$suffix""""
293	
294	  /** Convert all prerequisites, optionalPrerequisites, optionalPrerequisiteOf, and invalidates to a Graphviz
295	    * representation.
296	    * @param file the name of the output file
297	    */
298	  def dependenciesToGraphviz: String = {
299	
300	    def toGraphviz(digraph: DiGraph[B], attributes: String = "", tab: String = "    "): Option[String] = {
301	      val edges =
302	        digraph.getEdgeMap.collect { case (v, edges) if edges.nonEmpty => (v -> edges) }.map { case (v, edges) =>
303	          s"""${transformName(v)} -> ${edges.map(e => transformName(e)).mkString("{ ", " ", " }")}"""
304	        }
305	
306	      if (edges.isEmpty) { None }
307	      else {
308	        Some(
309	          s"""|  { $attributes
310	              |${edges.mkString(tab, "\n" + tab, "")}
311	              |  }""".stripMargin
312	        )
313	      }
314	    }
315	
316	    val connections =
317	      Seq(
318	        (prerequisiteGraph, "edge []"),
319	        (optionalPrerequisiteOfGraph, """edge [style=bold color="#4292c6"]"""),
320	        (invalidateGraph, """edge [minlen=2 style=dashed constraint=false color="#fb6a4a"]"""),
321	        (optionalPrerequisitesGraph, """edge [style=dotted color="#a1d99b"]""")
322	      ).flatMap { case (a, b) => toGraphviz(a, b) }
323	        .mkString("\n")
324	
325	    val nodes =
326	      (prerequisiteGraph + optionalPrerequisiteOfGraph + invalidateGraph + otherPrerequisites).getVertices
327	        .map(v => s"""${transformName(v)} [label="${v.name}"]""")
328	
329	    s"""|digraph DependencyManager {
330	        |  graph [rankdir=BT]
331	        |  node [fillcolor="${colormap(0)}",style=filled,shape=box]
332	        |${nodes.mkString("  ", "\n" + "  ", "")}
333	        |$connections
334	        |}
335	        |""".stripMargin
336	  }
337	
338	  def transformOrderToGraphviz(colormap: Seq[String] = colormap): String = {
339	
340	    def rotate[A](a: Seq[A]): Seq[A] = a match {
341	      case Nil        => Nil
342	      case car :: cdr => cdr :+ car
343	      case car        => car
344	    }
345	
346	    val sorted = ArrayBuffer.empty[String]
347	
348	    def rec(pm: DependencyManager[A, B], cm: Seq[String], tab: String = "", id: Int = 0): (String, Int) = {
349	      var offset = id
350	
351	      val targets = pm._targets.toSeq.map(_.name).mkString(", ")
352	      val state = pm._currentState.toSeq.map(_.name).mkString(", ")
353	
354	      val header = s"""|${tab}subgraph cluster_$id {
355	                       |$tab  label="targets: $targets\\nstate: $state"
356	                       |$tab  labeljust=l
357	                       |$tab  node [fillcolor="${cm.head}"]""".stripMargin
358	
359	      val body = pm.transformOrder.map {
360	        case a: DependencyManager[A, B] =>
361	          val (str, d) = rec(a, rotate(cm), tab + "  ", offset + 1)
362	          offset = d
363	          str
364	        case a =>
365	          val name = s"""${transformName(a, "_" + id)}"""
366	          sorted += name
367	          s"""$tab  $name [label="${a.name}"]"""
368	      }.mkString("\n")
369	
370	      (Seq(header, body, s"$tab}").mkString("\n"), offset)
371	    }
372	
373	    s"""|digraph DependencyManagerTransformOrder {
374	        |  graph [rankdir=TB]
375	        |  node [style=filled,shape=box]
376	        |${rec(this, colormap, "  ")._1}
377	        |  ${sorted.mkString(" -> ")}
378	        |}""".stripMargin
379	  }
380	
381	  /** A method that can be overridden to define custom print handling. This is useful if you would like to make some
382	    * transform print additional information.
383	    * @param tab the current tab setting
384	    * @param charSet the character set in use
385	    * @param size the number of nodes at the current level of the tree
386	    */
387	  def customPrintHandling(
388	    tab:     String,
389	    charSet: CharSet,
390	    size:    Int
391	  ): Option[PartialFunction[(B, Int), Seq[String]]] = None
392	
393	  /** Helper utility when recursing during pretty printing
394	    * @param tab an indentation string to use for every line of output
395	    * @param charSet a collection of characters to use when printing
396	    * @param preprocess a partial function that will be used before any other printing logic
397	    */
398	  def prettyPrintRec(tab: String, charSet: CharSet): Seq[String] = {
399	
400	    val (l, n, c) = (charSet.lastNode, charSet.notLastNode, charSet.continuation)
401	    val last = transformOrder.size - 1
402	
403	    val defaultHandling: PartialFunction[(B, Int), Seq[String]] = {
404	      case (a: DependencyManager[_, _], `last`) =>
405	        Seq(s"$tab$l ${a.name}") ++ a.prettyPrintRec(s"""$tab${" " * c.size} """, charSet)
406	      case (a: DependencyManager[_, _], _) => Seq(s"$tab$n ${a.name}") ++ a.prettyPrintRec(s"$tab$c ", charSet)
407	      case (a, `last`)                     => Seq(s"$tab$l ${a.name}")
408	      case (a, _)                          => Seq(s"$tab$n ${a.name}")
409	    }
410	
411	    val handling = customPrintHandling(tab, charSet, transformOrder.size) match {
412	      case Some(a) => a.orElse(defaultHandling)
413	      case None    => defaultHandling
414	    }
415	
416	    transformOrder.zipWithIndex
417	      .flatMap(handling)
418	  }
419	
420	  /** Textually show the determined transform order
421	    * @param tab an indentation string to use for every line of output
422	    * @param charSet a collection of characters to use when printing
423	    */
424	  def prettyPrint(
425	    tab:     String = "",
426	    charSet: DependencyManagerUtils.CharSet = DependencyManagerUtils.PrettyCharSet
427	  ): String = {
428	
429	    (Seq(s"$tab$name") ++ prettyPrintRec(tab, charSet)).mkString("\n")
430	
431	  }
432	
433	}
434	
435	/** A [[Phase]] that will ensure that some other [[Phase]]s and their prerequisites are executed.
436	  *
437	  * This tries to determine a phase ordering such that an [[AnnotationSeq]] ''output'' is produced that has had all of
438	  * the requested [[Phase]] target transforms run without having them be invalidated.
439	  * @param targets the [[Phase]]s you want to run
440	  */
441	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
442	class PhaseManager(
443	  val targets:      Seq[PhaseManager.PhaseDependency],
444	  val currentState: Seq[PhaseManager.PhaseDependency] = Seq.empty,
445	  val knownObjects: Set[Phase] = Set.empty
446	) extends DependencyManager[AnnotationSeq, Phase]
447	    with Phase {
448	
449	  import PhaseManager.PhaseDependency

451	}

453	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
454	object PhaseManager {
455	
456	  /** The type used to represent dependencies between [[Phase]]s */
457	  type PhaseDependency = Dependency[Phase]
458	
459	}

461	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
462	object DependencyManagerUtils {
463	
464	  /** A character set used for pretty printing
465	    * @see [[PrettyCharSet]]
466	    * @see [[ASCIICharSet]]
467	    */
468	  trait CharSet {
469	
470	    /** Used when printing the last node */
471	    val lastNode: String
472	
473	    /** Used when printing a node that is NOT the last */
474	    val notLastNode: String
475	
476	    /** Used while recursing into a node that is NOT the last */
477	    val continuation: String
478	  }
479	
480	  /** Uses prettier characters, but possibly not supported by all fonts */
481	  object PrettyCharSet extends CharSet {
482	    val lastNode = "└──"
483	    val notLastNode = "├──"
484	    val continuation = "│  "
485	  }
486	
487	  /** Basic ASCII output */
488	  object ASCIICharSet extends CharSet {
489	    val lastNode = "\\--"
490	    val notLastNode = "|--"
491	    val continuation = "|  "
492	  }
493	
494	}

3	package firrtl.options
4	
5	import firrtl.AnnotationSeq
6	
7	import logger.LazyLogging
8	
9	import scala.collection.mutable.LinkedHashSet
10	
11	import scala.reflect.ClassTag
12	import java.lang.reflect.Modifier
13	
14	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
15	object Dependency {
16	  def apply[A <: DependencyAPI[_]: ClassTag]: Dependency[A] = {
17	    val clazz = reflect.classTag[A].runtimeClass
18	    Dependency(Left(clazz.asInstanceOf[Class[A]]))
19	  }
20	
21	  def apply[A <: DependencyAPI[_]](c: Class[_ <: A]): Dependency[A] = {
22	    // It's forbidden to wrap the class of a singleton as a Dependency
23	    require(c.getName.last != '$')
24	    Dependency(Left(c))
25	  }
26	
27	  def apply[A <: DependencyAPI[_]](o: A with Singleton): Dependency[A] = Dependency(Right(o))
28	
29	  def fromTransform[A <: DependencyAPI[_]](t: A): Dependency[A] = {
30	    if (isSingleton(t)) {
31	      Dependency[A](Right(t.asInstanceOf[A with Singleton]))
32	    } else {
33	      Dependency[A](Left(t.getClass))
34	    }
35	  }
36	
37	  private def isSingleton(obj: Any): Boolean = {
38	    val clazz = obj.getClass
39	    try {
40	      // Check if the class has a static field named "MODULE$"
41	      val moduleField = clazz.getDeclaredField("MODULE$")
42	      Modifier.isStatic(moduleField.getModifiers)
43	    } catch {
44	      case _: NoSuchFieldException => false
45	    }
46	  }
47	}
48	
49	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
50	case class Dependency[+A <: DependencyAPI[_]](id: Either[Class[_ <: A], A with Singleton]) {
51	  def getObject(): A = id match {
52	    case Left(c)  => safeConstruct(c)
53	    case Right(o) => o
54	  }
55	
56	  def getSimpleName: String = id match {
57	    case Left(c)  => c.getSimpleName
58	    case Right(o) => o.getClass.getSimpleName
59	  }
60	
61	  def getName: String = id match {
62	    case Left(c)  => c.getName
63	    case Right(o) => o.getClass.getName
64	  }
65	
66	  /** Wrap an [[IllegalAccessException]] due to attempted object construction in a [[DependencyManagerException]] */
67	  private def safeConstruct[A](a: Class[_ <: A]): A = try { a.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance() }
68	  catch {
69	    case e: IllegalAccessException =>
70	      throw new DependencyManagerException(s"Failed to construct '$a'! (Did you try to construct an object?)", e)
71	    case e: InstantiationException =>
72	      throw new DependencyManagerException(
73	        s"Failed to construct '$a'! (Did you try to construct an inner class or a class with parameters?)",
74	        e
75	      )
76	  }
77	}

79	/** A polymorphic mathematical transform
80	  * @tparam A the transformed type
81	  */
82	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
83	trait TransformLike[A] extends LazyLogging {
84	
85	  /** An identifier of this [[TransformLike]] that can be used for logging and informational printing */
86	  def name: String
87	
88	  /** A mathematical transform on some type
89	    * @param a an input object
90	    * @return an output object of the same type
91	    */
92	  def transform(a: A): A
93	
94	}

96	/** Mix-in that makes a [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] guaranteed to be an identity function on some
97	  * type.
98	  * @tparam A the transformed type
99	  */
100	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
101	trait IdentityLike[A] { this: TransformLike[A] =>
102	
103	  /** The internal operation of this transform which, in order for this to be an identity function, must return nothing.
104	    * @param a an input object
105	    * @return nothing
106	    */
107	  protected def internalTransform(a: A): Unit = ()
108	
109	  /** This method will execute `internalTransform` and then return the original input object
110	    * @param a an input object
111	    * @return the input object
112	    */
113	  final override def transform(a: A): A = {
114	    internalTransform(a)
115	    a
116	  }
117	
118	}
119	
120	/** Mixin that defines dependencies between [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]s (hereafter referred to as
121	  * "transforms")
122	  *
123	  * This trait forms the basis of the Dependency API of the Chisel/FIRRTL Hardware Compiler Framework. Dependencies are
124	  * defined in terms of prerequisistes, optional prerequisites, optional prerequisites of, and invalidates. A
125	  * prerequisite is a transform that must run before this transform. An optional prerequisites is transform that should
126	  * run before this transform if the other transform is a target (or the prerequisite of a target). An optional
127	  * prerequisite of is an optional prerequisite injected into another transform. Finally, invalidates define the set of
128	  * transforms whose effects this transform undos/invalidates. (Invalidation then implies that a transform that is
129	  * invalidated by this transform and needed by another transform will need to be re-run.)
130	  *
131	  * This Dependency API only defines dependencies. A concrete [[DependencyManager]] is expected to be used to statically
132	  * resolve a linear ordering of transforms that satisfies dependency requirements.
133	  * @tparam A some transform
134	  * @define seqNote @note The use of a Seq here is to preserve input order. Internally, this will be converted to a private,
135	  * ordered Set.
136	  */
137	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
138	trait DependencyAPI[A <: DependencyAPI[A]] { this: TransformLike[_] =>
139	
140	  /** All transform that must run before this transform
141	    * $seqNote
142	    */
143	  def prerequisites:                        Seq[Dependency[A]] = Seq.empty
144	  private[options] lazy val _prerequisites: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[A]] = new LinkedHashSet() ++ prerequisites
145	
146	  /** All transforms that, if a prerequisite of *another* transform, will run before this transform.
147	    * $seqNote
148	    */
149	  def optionalPrerequisites: Seq[Dependency[A]] = Seq.empty
150	  private[options] lazy val _optionalPrerequisites: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[A]] =
151	    new LinkedHashSet() ++ optionalPrerequisites
152	
153	  /** A sequence of transforms to add this transform as an `optionalPrerequisite`. The use of `optionalPrerequisiteOf`
154	    * enables the transform declaring them to always run before some other transforms. However, declaring
155	    * `optionalPrerequisiteOf` will not result in the sequence of transforms executing.
156	    *
157	    * This is useful for providing an ordering constraint to guarantee that other transforms (e.g., emitters) will not
158	    * be scheduled before you.
159	    *
160	    * @note This method **will not** result in the listed transforms running. If you want to add multiple transforms at
161	    * once, you should use a `DependencyManager` with multiple targets.
162	    */
163	  def optionalPrerequisiteOf: Seq[Dependency[A]] = Seq.empty
164	  private[options] lazy val _optionalPrerequisiteOf: LinkedHashSet[Dependency[A]] =
165	    new LinkedHashSet() ++ optionalPrerequisiteOf
166	
167	  /** A function that, given *another* transform (parameter `a`) will return true if this transform invalidates/undos the
168	    * effects of the *other* transform (parameter `a`).
169	    * @param a transform
170	    */
171	  def invalidates(a: A): Boolean = true
172	
173	}
174	
175	/** A mathematical transformation of an [[AnnotationSeq]].
176	  *
177	  * A [[firrtl.options.Phase Phase]] forms one unit in the Chisel/FIRRTL Hardware Compiler Framework (HCF). The HCF is
178	  * built from a sequence of [[firrtl.options.Phase Phase]]s applied to an [[AnnotationSeq]]. Note that a
179	  * [[firrtl.options.Phase Phase]] may consist of multiple phases internally.
180	  */
181	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
182	trait Phase extends TransformLike[AnnotationSeq] with DependencyAPI[Phase] {
183	
184	  /** The name of this [[firrtl.options.Phase Phase]]. This will be used to generate debug/error messages or when deleting
185	    * annotations. This will default to the `simpleName` of the class.
186	    * @return this phase's name
187	    * @note Override this with your own implementation for different naming behavior.
188	    */
189	  lazy val name: String = this.getClass.getName
190	
191	}
192	
193	/** A [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] that internally ''translates'' the input type to some other type,
194	  * transforms the internal type, and converts back to the original type.
195	  *
196	  * This is intended to be used to insert a [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]] parameterized by type `B`
197	  * into a sequence of [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]s parameterized by type `A`.
198	  * @tparam A the type of the [[firrtl.options.TransformLike TransformLike]]
199	  * @tparam B the internal type
200	  */
201	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
202	trait Translator[A, B] extends TransformLike[A] {
203	
204	  /** A method converting type `A` into type `B`
205	    * @param an object of type `A`
206	    * @return an object of type `B`
207	    */
208	  protected implicit def aToB(a: A): B
209	
210	  /** A method converting type `B` back into type `A`
211	    * @param an object of type `B`
212	    * @return an object of type `A`
213	    */
214	  protected implicit def bToA(b: B): A
215	
216	  /** A transform on an internal type
217	    * @param b an object of type `B`
218	    * @return an object of type `B`
219	    */
220	  protected def internalTransform(b: B): B
221	
222	  /** Convert the input object to the internal type, transform the internal type, and convert back to the original type
223	    */
224	  override final def transform(a: A): A = bToA(internalTransform(aToB(a)))
225	
226	}

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package firrtl.options
4	
5	import firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, seqToAnnoSeq, AnnotationSeq}
6	import firrtl.annotations.Annotation
7	
8	import logger.Logger
9	
10	/** A [[Stage]] represents one stage in the FIRRTL hardware compiler framework. A [[Stage]] is, conceptually, a
11	  * [[Phase]] that includes a command line interface.
12	  *
13	  * The FIRRTL compiler is a stage as well as any frontend or backend that runs before/after FIRRTL. Concretely, Chisel
14	  * is a [[Stage]] as is FIRRTL's Verilog emitter. Each stage performs a mathematical transformation on an
15	  * [[AnnotationSeq]] where some input annotations are processed to produce different annotations. Command line options
16	  * may be pulled in if available.
17	  */
18	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
19	abstract class Stage extends Phase {
20	
21	  /** A utility that helps convert command line options to annotations */
22	  val shell: Shell
23	
24	  /** Run this stage on some input annotations
25	    * @param annotations input annotations
26	    * @return output annotations
27	    */
28	  protected def run(annotations: AnnotationSeq): AnnotationSeq
29	
30	  /** Execute this stage on some input annotations. Annotations will be read from any input annotation files.
31	    * @param annotations input annotations
32	    * @return output annotations
33	    * @throws firrtl.options.OptionsException if command line or annotation validation fails
34	    */
35	  @deprecated("Use the form that taks Seq[Annotation]", "Chisel 7.1.0")
36	  final def transform(annotations: AnnotationSeq): AnnotationSeq = {
37	    seqToAnnoSeq(transform(annotations.toSeq))
38	  }
39	
40	  /** Execute this stage on some input annotations. Annotations will be read from any input annotation files.
41	    * @param annotations input annotations
42	    * @return output annotations
43	    * @throws firrtl.options.OptionsException if command line or annotation validation fails
44	    */
45	  final def transform(annotations: Seq[Annotation]): Seq[Annotation] = {
46	    val annotationsx =
47	      Seq(new phases.GetIncludes)
48	        .foldLeft(annotations)((a, p) => p.transform(a))
49	
50	    Logger.makeScope(annotationsx) {
51	      Seq(
52	        new phases.AddDefaults,
53	        new phases.Checks,
54	        new Phase { def transform(a: AnnotationSeq) = run(a) },
55	        new phases.WriteOutputAnnotations
56	      )
57	        .foldLeft(annotationsx)((a, p) => p.transform(a))
58	    }
59	  }
60	
61	  /** Run this stage on on a mix of arguments and annotations
62	    * @param args command line arguments
63	    * @param initialAnnotations annotation
64	    * @return output annotations
65	    * @throws firrtl.options.OptionsException if command line or annotation validation fails
66	    */
67	  @deprecated("Use the form that taks Seq[Annotation]", "Chisel 7.1.0")
68	  final def execute(args: Array[String], annotations: AnnotationSeq): AnnotationSeq =
69	    seqToAnnoSeq(execute(args, annotations.toSeq))
70	
71	  /** Run this stage on on a mix of arguments and annotations
72	    * @param args command line arguments
73	    * @param initialAnnotations annotation
74	    * @return output annotations
75	    * @throws firrtl.options.OptionsException if command line or annotation validation fails
76	    */
77	  final def execute(args: Array[String], annotations: Seq[Annotation]): Seq[Annotation] =
78	    transform(shell.parse(args, annotations))
79	}
80	
81	/** Provides a main method for a [[Stage]]
82	  * @param stage the stage to run
83	  */
84	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
85	class StageMain(val stage: Stage) {
86	
87	  /** The main function that serves as this stage's command line interface.
88	    * @param args command line arguments
89	    */
90	  final def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = try {
91	    stage.execute(args, Seq.empty)
92	  } catch {
93	    case a: StageError =>
94	      System.exit(a.code.number)
95	    case a: OptionsException =>
96	      StageUtils.dramaticUsageError(a.message)
97	      System.exit(1)
98	  }
99	
100	}
101

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package firrtl.options
4	
5	import firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, seqToAnnoSeq, AnnotationSeq}
6	
7	import logger.{ClassLogLevelAnnotation, LogClassNamesAnnotation, LogFileAnnotation, LogLevelAnnotation}
8	
9	import scopt.OptionParser
10	
11	import java.util.ServiceLoader
12	
13	/** A utility for working with command line options.  This provides no options by default other than "--help".  This is
14	  * intended for lower-level APIs which do not want to include options that are provided by [[Shell]].
15	  *
16	  * @param applicationName
17	  */
18	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
19	class BareShell(val applicationName: String) {
20	
21	  /** Command line argument parser (OptionParser) with modifications */
22	  protected val parser = new OptionParser[AnnotationSeq](applicationName) with DuplicateHandling with ExceptOnError
23	  parser.help("help").text("prints this usage text")
24	
25	  /** This method can be overriden to do some work everytime before parsing runs, e.g., to add options to the parser. */
26	  protected def parserSetup(): Unit = {}
27	
28	  /** The [[AnnotationSeq]] generated from command line arguments
29	    *
30	    * This requires lazy evaluation as subclasses will mixin new command
31	    * line options via methods of [[Shell.parser]]
32	    */
33	  def parse(args: Array[String], initAnnos: AnnotationSeq = Seq.empty): AnnotationSeq = {
34	    parserSetup()
35	    parser
36	      .parse(args, initAnnos.reverse)
37	      .getOrElse(throw new OptionsException("Failed to parse command line options", new IllegalArgumentException))
38	      .reverse
39	  }
40	
41	}
42	
43	/** A utility for working with command line options.  This comes prepopulated with common options for most uses.
44	  *
45	  * @param applicationName the application associated with these command line options
46	  */
47	@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")
48	class Shell(applicationName: String) extends BareShell(applicationName) {
49	
50	  /** Contains all discovered [[RegisteredLibrary]] */
51	  final lazy val registeredLibraries: Seq[RegisteredLibrary] = {
52	    val libraries = scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer[RegisteredLibrary]()
53	    val iter = ServiceLoader.load(classOf[RegisteredLibrary]).iterator()
54	    while (iter.hasNext) {
55	      val lib = iter.next()
56	      libraries += lib
57	      parser.note(lib.name)
58	      lib.addOptions(parser)
59	    }
60	
61	    libraries.toSeq
62	  }
63	
64	  override protected def parserSetup(): Unit = {
65	    if (sys.env.get("CHISEL_ARGUMENT_EXTENSIONS") != Some("DISABLE")) {
66	      registeredLibraries
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  parser.note("Shell Options")
71	  ProgramArgsAnnotation.addOptions(parser)
72	  Seq(TargetDirAnnotation, InputAnnotationFileAnnotation, OutputAnnotationFileAnnotation)
73	    .foreach(_.addOptions(parser))
74	
75	  parser
76	    .opt[Unit]("show-registrations")
77	    .action { (_, c) =>
78	      val rlString = registeredLibraries.map(l => s"\n  - ${l.getClass.getName}").mkString
79	
80	      println(s"""The following libraries registered command line options:$rlString""")
81	      c
82	    }
83	    .unbounded()
84	    .text("print discovered registered libraries and transforms")
85	
86	  parser.note("Logging Options")
87	  Seq(LogLevelAnnotation, ClassLogLevelAnnotation, LogFileAnnotation, LogClassNamesAnnotation)
88	    .foreach(_.addOptions(parser))
89	}

37	case class PreserveAggregate(mode: PreserveAggregate.Type) extends NoTargetAnnotation with CIRCTOption
38	
39	/** Object storing types associated with different CIRCT target languages, e.g., RTL or SystemVerilog */
40	object CIRCTTarget {
41	
42	  /** The parent type of all CIRCT targets */
43	  sealed trait Type
44	
45	  /** Specification FIRRTL */
46	  case object CHIRRTL extends Type
47	
48	  /** The FIRRTL dialect */
49	  case object FIRRTL extends Type
50	
51	  /** The HW dialect */
52	  case object HW extends Type
53	
54	  /** The Verilog language */
55	  case object Verilog extends Type
56	
57	  /** The SystemVerilog language */
58	  case object SystemVerilog extends Type
59	
60	  /* The btor2 format for bounded model checking */
61	  case object Btor2 extends Type
62	}
63	
64	/** Annotation that tells [[circt.stage.phases.CIRCT CIRCT]] what target to compile to */
65	case class CIRCTTargetAnnotation(target: CIRCTTarget.Type) extends NoTargetAnnotation with CIRCTOption
66	
67	object CIRCTTargetAnnotation extends HasShellOptions {
68	
69	  override def options = Seq(
70	    new ShellOption[String](
71	      longOption = "target",
72	      toAnnotationSeq = _ match {
73	        case "chirrtl"       => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.CHIRRTL))
74	        case "firrtl"        => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.FIRRTL))
75	        case "hw"            => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.HW))
76	        case "verilog"       => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.Verilog))
77	        case "systemverilog" => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.SystemVerilog))
78	        case "btor2"         => Seq(CIRCTTargetAnnotation(CIRCTTarget.Btor2))
79	        case a               => throw new OptionsException(s"Unknown target name '$a'! (Did you misspell it?)")
80	      },
81	      helpText = "The CIRCT",
82	      helpValueName = Some("{chirrtl|firrtl|hw|verilog|systemverilog|btor2}")
83	    )
84	  )
85	
86	}
87	
88	/** Annotation holding an emitted MLIR string
89	  *
90	  * @param filename the name of the file where this should be written
91	  * @param value a string of MLIR
92	  * @param suffix an optional suffix added to the filename when this is written to disk
93	  */
94	case class EmittedMLIR(filename: String, value: String, suffix: Option[String])
95	    extends NoTargetAnnotation
96	    with CustomFileEmission {
97	
98	  override protected def baseFileName(annotations: AnnotationSeq): String = filename
99	
100	  override def getBytes = value.getBytes
101	
102	}

104	private[stage] case object FirtoolBinaryPath extends HasShellOptions {
105	  override def options = Seq(
106	    new ShellOption(
107	      longOption = "firtool-binary-path",
108	      toAnnotationSeq = (path: String) => Seq(FirtoolBinaryPath(path)),
109	      helpText = """Specifies the path to the "firtool" binary Chisel should use.""",
110	      helpValueName = Some("path")
111	    )
112	  )
113	}
114	
115	/** Annotation that tells [[circt.stage.phases.CIRCT CIRCT]] what firtool executable to use */

val delayFilter = Module(new FirFilter(8, Seq(0.U, 1.U)))  // 1-cycle delay as a FIR filter
val triangleFilter = Module(new FirFilter(8, Seq(1.U, 2.U, 3.U, 2.U, 1.U)))  // 5-point FIR filter with a triangle impulse response
```

The above can be converted to Verilog using `ChiselStage`:

```scala
import chisel3.stage.ChiselGeneratorAnnotation
import circt.stage.{ChiselStage, FirtoolOption}

(new ChiselStage).execute(
  Array("--target", "systemverilog"),
  Seq(ChiselGeneratorAnnotation(() => new FirFilter(8, Seq(1.U, 1.U, 1.U))),
    FirtoolOption("--disable-all-randomization"))
)
```

Alternatively, you may generate some Verilog directly for inspection:

```scala
val verilogString = chisel3.getVerilogString(new FirFilter(8, Seq(0.U, 1.U)))
println(verilogString)
```

**No setup is required** (it runs in the browser), nor does it assume any prior knowledge of Scala.

# Stage / Phase 管道

## 1. 本讲目标

## 3. 本讲源码地图
## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

  ### 4.2 Phase 与 DependencyAPI：用依赖关系代替写死的顺序
  ### 4.3 PhaseManager：把依赖关系拓扑排序成执行顺序
  ### 4.4 Shell 与 CLI：命令行如何变成注解
  ### 4.5 AnnotationSeq：贯穿整条管道的数据载体
## 5. 综合实践
## 6. 本讲小结
## 7. 下一步学习建议

That's 4 modules. Let me design the 4.x sections around these. I'll structure:
- 4.1 circt.stage.ChiselStage（含 ChiselStage.run / phase 两条管道）
- 4.2 Phase / DependencyAPI（prerequisites/invalidates 依赖机制）
- 4.3 PhaseManager（拓扑排序 transformOrder）
- 4.4 Shell / CLI（命令行→注解）
- 4.5 AnnotationSeq（数据载体 + ChiselOptionsView）

Let me note key accurate facts:
- ChiselStage.run uses PhaseManager with 8 targets and currentState containing AddDefaults + Checks (firrtl.stage.phases)
- The companion `ChiselStage.phase` is a different PhaseManager with 8 targets: Elaborate, AddDebugIntrinsics, Convert, AddDedupGroupAnnotations, circt.AddImplicitOutputFile, chisel3.AddImplicitOutputAnnotationFile, circt.Checks, CIRCT
- Note: the companion's `phase` includes `Elaborate` as a target, while the `ChiselStage` class's `run` does NOT include Elaborate directly (because when you use `(new ChiselStage).execute`, the Stage framework adds Elaborate via... actually no. Let me re-check.)

Wait, this is an important subtlety. Let me re-examine.

In `class ChiselStage.run`, the targets are:
- AddImplicitOutputFile (chisel3)
- AddImplicitOutputAnnotationFile (chisel3)
- AddSerializationAnnotations
- AddDebugIntrinsics
- Convert
- AddDedupGroupAnnotations
- AddImplicitOutputFile (circt)
- CIRCT

Notably, Elaborate is NOT in the `run` targets. But Convert has prerequisite Elaborate. So when PhaseManager resolves the dependency graph, it will pull in Elaborate (and its prerequisites) automatically. So Elaborate runs because it's a prerequisite of Convert, not because it's a direct target. That's the elegance of the dependency-based system.

The companion's `phase` includes Elaborate explicitly as a target, plus AddDebugIntrinsics, Convert, AddDedupGroupAnnotations, circt.AddImplicitOutputFile, chisel3.AddImplicitOutputAnnotationFile, circt.Checks, CIRCT.

Interesting difference: the companion `phase` does NOT include AddSerializationAnnotations or chisel3.AddImplicitOutputFile as targets, but `run` does. The companion phase includes circt.Checks as a target, while `run` relies on currentState.

This is a great teaching point — the two PhaseManagers are slightly different but converge because the dependency graph auto-pulls prerequisites.

Now let me also verify: in `class ChiselStage`, `currentState = Seq(AddDefaults, Checks)` where these are `firrtl.stage.phases`. But the `transform` in Stage.scala already wraps run with AddDefaults and Checks... Hmm, actually there's overlap. Let me not over-explain this; the key teaching point is the targets + currentState + dependency resolution.

Actually, the `currentState` tells the PhaseManager "these phases have already run / their effects are already present, don't schedule them or their prerequisites again." So `AddDefaults` and firrtl `Checks` are treated as already-done. This matters because the Stage.transform already runs AddDefaults+Checks+GetIncludes+WriteOutputAnnotations around run.

Let me also confirm the prerequisite chains:
- Elaborate → prerequisites: chisel3.stage.phases.Checks, logger.phases.Checks
- Convert → prerequisite: Elaborate
- CIRCT → prerequisite: circt.stage.phases.AddImplicitOutputFile
- circt.Checks → optionalPrerequisiteOf: CIRCT (so it runs before CIRCT if CIRCT is in the graph)

input.content:
# Stage / Phase 管道

## 1. 本讲目标

本讲是单元 5「Stage 与 CIRCT 集成」的首篇，承接 u1-l5 给出的四段式架构（前端 → Builder → 内部 IR → 发射），打开其中最后一段「如何把这些阶段串成一条管道」的机制。

读完本讲你应当能够：

1. 读懂 `circt.stage.ChiselStage` 的两条管道：`class ChiselStage.run`（命令行入口）与 `object ChiselStage.phase`（库 API 入口），并指出它们的差别与共性。
2. 说清 `Phase` 是什么，以及 `prerequisites` / `optionalPrerequisites` / `optionalPrerequisiteOf` / `invalidates` 这四组依赖关系如何**取代写死的顺序**来描述管道。
3. 理解 `PhaseManager` 如何把「一组依赖关系」拓扑排序成一个可执行的 `Phase` 线性序列，并据此自动拉入未声明的必需阶段（如 `Elaborate`）。
4. 说清 `Shell` / `CLI` 如何把命令行字符串（如 `--target systemverilog`）解析成 `Annotation`，以及 `AnnotationSeq` 如何作为唯一的数据载体贯穿整条管道。

> 本讲只讲「管道怎么搭」，具体每个阶段内部做什么分别留给 u5-l2（Elaborate）、u5-l3（Convert/Checks/Emitter）、u5-l4（CIRCT/firtool）。

## 2. 前置知识

在进入源码前，先建立三个直觉。它们来自 u1-l5，这里做一句话回顾并补充本讲要用到的新术语。

### 2.1 「注解流」而非「数据流」

很多编译器把中间表示（IR）从头传到尾，每个 pass 加工 IR。Chisel/FIRRTL 的硬件编译框架（HCF, Hardware Compiler Framework）走的是另一条路：**所有阶段共享的唯一数据结构是一个注解列表 `AnnotationSeq`，即 `Seq[Annotation]`**。每个阶段读入一批注解、产出另一批注解；电路本身（`ElaboratedCircuit`、`firrtl.ir.Circuit`、生成的 Verilog 字符串）也都以**特殊注解**的形式被塞进这个列表里传来传去。

这种设计的好处是阶段之间**极度松耦合**：阶段 A 不需要知道阶段 B 的存在，只需要认得自己关心的那几类注解。代价是你得习惯「数据全藏在注解里」。

### 2.2 阶段（Phase）：一个数学变换

一个 `Phase` 在数学上就是 `AnnotationSeq => AnnotationSeq` 的一个函数（源码里叫 `TransformLike`）。它不做 I/O 副作用以外的事，输入输出同类型。把若干 `Phase` 串起来 `foldLeft`，就是一条编译管道。

### 2.3 关键术语表

| 术语 | 含义 | 首次出现 |
|------|------|----------|
| `Annotation` | 一条带类型标签的元数据，是管道里流动的「数据包」 | §4.5 |
| `AnnotationSeq` | `Seq[Annotation]`，贯穿全管道的唯一数据载体 | §4.5 |
| `Phase` | 对 `AnnotationSeq` 的一次数学变换，编译管道的一个「小步骤」 | §4.2 |
| `Dependency` | 对某个 `Phase` 的引用（按类名或单例对象），用于声明依赖关系 | §4.2 |
| `PhaseManager` | 读入一组 `Phase` 依赖关系，拓扑排序出执行顺序的调度器 | §4.3 |
| `Stage` | 带命令行界面的 `Phase`（「一个能从命令行驱动的阶段」） | §4.1 |
| `Shell` / `CLI` | 命令行选项解析器，把 `--xxx` 字符串变成 `Annotation` | §4.4 |

## 3. 本讲源码地图

本讲涉及的关键文件，按「从用户入口到底层机制」排列：

| 文件 | 作用 |
|------|------|
| [src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala) | 用户入口。`class ChiselStage`（命令行）与 `object ChiselStage`（库 API，如 `emitSystemVerilog`），各自组装一条 `PhaseManager` 管道。 |
| [src/main/scala/circt/stage/Shell.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/Shell.scala) | `CLI` trait，声明 Chisel/CIRCT 的全部命令行选项。 |
| [src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/package.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/package.scala) | `ChiselOptionsView`：把散落的注解「投影」成一个结构化的 `ChiselOptions` 配置对象。 |
| [src/main/scala/circt/stage/Annotations.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/Annotations.scala) | `CIRCTTargetAnnotation`、`FirtoolOption` 等注解定义及其 `--target` / `--firtool-option` 选项绑定。 |
| [firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala) | `Phase`、`DependencyAPI`、`Dependency` 的定义——依赖机制的根基。 |
| [firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/DependencyManager.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/DependencyManager.scala) | `PhaseManager`：拓扑排序、`transformOrder`、执行引擎。 |
| [firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Stage.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Stage.scala) | `Stage` 抽象基类与 `StageMain`（命令行 `main` 方法）。 |
| [firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Shell.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Shell.scala) | `BareShell` / `Shell`：基于 scopt 的命令行解析骨架。 |
| [src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/Elaborate.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/Elaborate.scala) | `Elaborate` 阶段示例：看它如何声明 `prerequisites`。 |

> 提示：`firrtl.*` 包下的 API 在源码里整体标注了 `@deprecated("All APIs in package firrtl are deprecated.", "Chisel 7.0.0")`，但它们**目前仍是 Chisel 编译管道的实际实现**（见后续模块说明）。这里的 deprecated 是「将来要从 firrtl 命名空间迁出」，不是「已废弃勿用」，学习阶段照常阅读。

---

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

本讲覆盖四个最小模块：

- **4.1** `circt.stage.ChiselStage`：用户入口与两条管道
- **4.2** `Phase` 与 `DependencyAPI`：用依赖关系代替写死的顺序
- **4.3** `PhaseManager`：把依赖关系拓扑排序成执行顺序
- **4.4** `Shell` 与 CLI：命令行如何变成注解
- **4.5** `AnnotationSeq`：贯穿整条管道的数据载体

### 4.1 circt.stage.ChiselStage：用户入口与两条管道

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

`circt.stage.ChiselStage` 是现代 Chisel 生成 Verilog 的**统一入口**。它有两副面孔：

- **`class ChiselStage`**：一个「Stage」（带命令行的阶段），供命令行脚本或 `(new ChiselStage).execute(...)` 调用。它的核心方法是 `run`，在里面用 `PhaseManager` 搭出管道。
- **`object ChiselStage`**（伴生对象）：一组更友好的库 API，如 `emitSystemVerilog`、`emitCHIRRTL`、`elaborate`。它们各自组装一条**独立的** `PhaseManager`（见私有方法 `phase`），然后把结果注解 `collectFirst` 出来。

之所以有两条管道，是因为命令行入口和库 API 的需求略有不同（例如库 API 通常想直接拿到字符串结果，命令行入口想写文件）。但它们都用同一个机制——`PhaseManager`——只是往里塞的 `targets`（目标阶段）集合不同。

> 历史提示：`chisel3.stage` 包里有一个旧入口 `chisel3.stage.ChiselStage`。源码里专门留了一条提示字符串 `pleaseSwitchToCIRCT`，告诉大家改用 `circt.stage.ChiselStage`。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

`class ChiselStage` 的工作流程：

1. 收到一组输入 `AnnotationSeq`（来自命令行解析或调用方）。
2. 在 `run` 里 `new PhaseManager(targets = ..., currentState = ...)`。
3. `pm.transform(annotations)` 让 `PhaseManager` 自动拓扑排序并依次执行各阶段。
4. 返回变换后的 `AnnotationSeq`。

`object ChiselStage.emitSystemVerilog` 的工作流程：

1. 用 `ChiselGeneratorAnnotation(() => gen)` 告诉管道「要细化哪个模块」，用 `CIRCTTargetAnnotation(SystemVerilog)` 告诉管道「目标是 SystemVerilog」。
2. 用 `(new Shell("circt")).parse(args)` 把额外命令行参数也变成注解；用 `firtoolOpts.map(FirtoolOption(_))` 把 firtool 选项变成注解。
3. `phase.transform(annos)` 跑另一条 `PhaseManager` 管道。
4. 从结果注解里 `collectFirst { case EmittedVerilogCircuitAnnotation(a) => a }.get.value` 取出 Verilog 字符串。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

先看 `class ChiselStage`——注意它 `extends Stage`，且声明「自己不依赖任何阶段、不使任何阶段失效」：

[ChiselStage.scala:18-28](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L18-L28) —— `class ChiselStage extends Stage`，并重写 `shell` 为带 `CLI` 的 Shell。这里 `Stage` 是「带命令行的 Phase」（见 §4.2）。

`run` 方法是命令行管道的组装处：

[ChiselStage.scala:30-49](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L30-L49) —— `run` 构造一个 `PhaseManager`。要点：

- `targets`（第 33–42 行）是「你想要最终跑完的阶段」，共 8 个：`AddImplicitOutputFile`、`AddImplicitOutputAnnotationFile`、`AddSerializationAnnotations`、`AddDebugIntrinsics`、`Convert`、`AddDedupGroupAnnotations`、`circt.stage.phases.AddImplicitOutputFile`、`CIRCT`。
- 注意 `targets` 里**没有** `Elaborate`！但 `Convert` 声明了 `prerequisites = [Elaborate]`，所以 `PhaseManager` 会**自动把 `Elaborate` 及其前提拉进来**。这就是依赖式调度的威力（详见 §4.3）。
- `currentState`（第 43–46 行）是「假定已经跑过、不必再跑」的阶段：`firrtl.stage.phases.AddDefaults` 与 `firrtl.stage.phases.Checks`。
- 第 48 行 `pm.transform(annotations)` 真正驱动整条管道。

再看伴生对象的库 API 管道——它与上面的 `run` 管道**不同**：

[ChiselStage.scala:56-68](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L56-L68) —— 私有方法 `phase` 构造**另一条** `PhaseManager`，它的 `targets` 显式包含 `Elaborate`，还包含 `circt.stage.phases.Checks`，但不包含 `AddSerializationAnnotations`、`chisel3.AddImplicitOutputFile`。两条管道目标不同，但因为依赖图会自动补全前提，最终执行到的阶段集合高度重合。

`emitSystemVerilog` 是这条管道的典型消费者：

[ChiselStage.scala:197-213](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L197-L213) —— 组装初始注解（生成器 + 目标 + 命令行 + firtool 选项），`phase.transform` 跑完，再 `collectFirst` 抓出 `EmittedVerilogCircuitAnnotation` 的 `.value`（即 Verilog 字符串）。这就是 u1-l4 里「按下生成按钮的那一行」落到源码上的样子。

命令行入口由 `ChiselMain` 提供：

[ChiselStage.scala:258-259](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L258-L259) —— `object ChiselMain extends StageMain(new ChiselStage)`。`StageMain` 给一个 `Stage` 套上标准 `main(args)`（详见 §4.4），于是 `ChiselMain` 就成了一个可执行程序的入口。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践

**实践目标**：确认「库 API」与「命令行」两条管道确实存在且目标集合不同。

**操作步骤**：

1. 打开 [ChiselStage.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala)。
2. 找到 `class ChiselStage` 的 `run`（第 30 行）与 `object ChiselStage` 的 `phase`（第 57 行）。
3. 把两个 `targets` 列表逐条抄下来，画一张对照表（左列「`run` 管道」、右列「`phase` 管道」），用 ✅/❌ 标出某阶段是否在各自 `targets` 里。
4. 用笔圈出：哪条管道**显式**列了 `Elaborate`？哪条没有？（答案：`phase` 显式列了，`run` 没有。）

**需要观察的现象**：两条管道的 `targets` 既不完全相同，也不完全互斥——它们只是从不同角度描述「我想要的最终产物」。

**预期结果**：你能给出一张表，说明 `Convert`、`CIRCT` 在两条管道都出现；`Elaborate`、`circt.Checks` 只在 `phase` 显式出现；`AddSerializationAnnotations`、`chisel3.AddImplicitOutputFile` 只在 `run` 出现。

> 若想验证「`run` 没列 `Elaborate` 但它仍然会跑」，可在 §4.3 的实践中用日志确认。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：为什么 `class ChiselStage.run` 的 `targets` 里不写 `Elaborate`，elaboration 却依然会发生？

**参考答案**：因为 `Convert` 阶段声明了 `prerequisites = Seq(Dependency[Elaborate])`（见 [Convert.scala:19](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/Convert.scala#L19)）。`PhaseManager` 解析依赖图时会自动把 `Elaborate`（及其前提 `chisel3.Checks`、`logger.Checks`）补进执行序列。`targets` 只表达「最终想要的」，前提由依赖关系自动推导。

**练习 2**：`emitSystemVerilog` 与 `(new ChiselStage).execute(...)` 用的是同一个 `PhaseManager` 吗？

**参考答案**：不是。前者用伴生对象的私有 `phase`（[ChiselStage.scala:57](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L57)），后者用 `class ChiselStage.run` 内构造的 `PhaseManager`（[ChiselStage.scala:32](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/ChiselStage.scala#L32)）。两者 `targets` 不同，但因依赖自动补全，实际执行到的阶段集合大体一致。

### 4.2 Phase 与 DependencyAPI：用依赖关系代替写死的顺序

#### 4.2.1 概念说明

最朴素的搭管道方式是写一个 `Seq[Phase]` 然后 `foldLeft`——但这样**顺序写死、刚性**：你想插一个新阶段，得手动找它在列表里的位置，还得保证它依赖的阶段排在前面。

HCF 用了一套更弹性的方案：**每个阶段只声明「我需要谁先跑」「我使谁失效」，由调度器去算出合法顺序**。这套声明的接口叫 `DependencyAPI`，承载它的抽象叫 `Phase`。

四个声明维度（直接引自源码注释）：

| 声明 | 语义 | 何时触发调度 |
|------|------|-------------|
| `prerequisites` | 必须在我**之前**跑的阶段（硬依赖） | 总是会被拉入并排在我之前 |
| `optionalPrerequisites` | 如果它在图里，就应排在我之前（软依赖） | 仅当该阶段已是图中节点时才生效 |
| `optionalPrerequisiteOf` | 把我自己注入为别人的软依赖（「我想排在 X 之前」） | 不会让 X 被拉入图，只施加顺序约束 |
| `invalidates(a)` | 我会**撤销** `a` 的效果（默认 `true`） | 若被撤销的 `a` 仍被下游需要，则 `a` 需要重跑 |

> 默认 `invalidates(a) = true` 意味着「一个阶段默认会推翻其他所有阶段的成果」。Chisel 自己的 `Phase` 几乎都把 `invalidates` 重写为 `false`（即「我跑完不影响别人」），因为它们都只是在往 `AnnotationSeq` 里追加注解，互不破坏。

#### 4.2.2 核心流程

`Phase` 的依赖模型可以用一张有向图理解：

- 节点 = `Phase`。
- 边 = 「A 是 B 的 prerequisite」⇒ 有一条 A → B 的边（A 必须先跑）。
- 调度 = 对这张图做**拓扑排序**，得到一个线性执行序列（DAG 的 `linearize`）。
- 冲突 = 图里有环 ⇒ 抛 `DependencyManagerException`。

一个 `Phase` 对外其实只有一个真正干活的方法：`transform(a: A): A`（来自 `TransformLike`）。四个依赖声明只是**元数据**，调度器读它们来排序，排序完了才挨个调 `transform`。

#### 4.2.3 源码精读

先看 `Phase` 的根基 `TransformLike`——它定义了「一个数学变换」：

[Phase.scala:82-94](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala#L82-L94) —— `trait TransformLike[A]` 只有两个抽象成员：`name`（用于日志）和 `transform(a: A): A`。这就是「阶段 = 一个纯函数」的最简定义。

`DependencyAPI` 在此之上加四组依赖声明：

[Phase.scala:137-173](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala#L137-L173) —— 四个方法 + 四个 `_` 前缀的内部缓存版本（`_prerequisites` 等，懒求值为 `LinkedHashSet`）。注意第 171 行 `def invalidates(a: A): Boolean = true`：**默认使所有其他阶段失效**。

`Phase` 把两者合体，并固定变换类型为 `AnnotationSeq`：

[Phase.scala:181-191](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala#L181-L191) —— `trait Phase extends TransformLike[AnnotationSeq] with DependencyAPI[Phase]`。`name` 默认取类全名。所以你写 `class Elaborate extends Phase`，就自动得到一个带依赖元数据的 `AnnotationSeq => AnnotationSeq` 变换。

依赖关系用 `Dependency` 类型来引用阶段，它本质上「按类名指向一个类」：

[Phase.scala:49-77](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/firrtl/src/main/scala/firrtl/options/Phase.scala#L49-L77) —— `case class Dependency[+A](id: Either[Class[_], A with Singleton])`，`getObject()` 通过反射 `newInstance` 构造（`Left`）或直接取单例（`Right`）。这就是为什么 `Dependency[Elaborate]` 能在运行时实例化一个 `Elaborate`。

来看一个真实 `Phase` 是怎么写依赖的——`Elaborate`：

[Elaborate.scala:29-37](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/Elaborate.scala#L29-L37) —— `prerequisites` 写了 `chisel3.stage.phases.Checks` 和 `logger.phases.Checks`（硬依赖），`invalidates(a) = false`（不破坏别人）。`Elaborate` 内部具体做什么留给 u5-l2，本讲只关注它「如何声明依赖」。

再看一个用 `optionalPrerequisiteOf` 的例子——`circt.stage.phases.Checks`：

[Checks.scala:13-17](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/phases/Checks.scala#L13-L17) —— `optionalPrerequisiteOf = Seq(Dependency[circt.stage.phases.CIRCT])`。意思是「只要 CIRCT 在图里，就把我排在 CIRCT 之前做合法性检查」。注意它**不会**让 CIRCT 被拉入图，只是施加顺序约束。

#### 4.2.4 代码实践

**实践目标**：亲手读懂一个真实 `Phase` 的四组依赖声明，判断它的「调度行为」。

**操作步骤**：

1. 打开 [CIRCT.scala](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/circt/stage/phases/CIRCT.scala)，定位 `class CIRCT extends Phase`（第 124 行）及其重写的依赖（第 130–135 行）。
2. 同样读 [AddDedupGroupAnnotations.scala:15-19](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/AddDedupGroupAnnotations.scala#L15-L19) 与 [AddDebugIntrinsics.scala:14-18](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/blob/b2a0e030da9a3e90b8221436b5317afb370f3347/src/main/scala/chisel3/stage/phases/AddDebugIntrinsics.scala#L14-L18)。
3. 填一张表：每个阶段，`prerequisites` / `optionalPrerequisites` / `optionalPrerequisiteOf` / `invalidates` 各填什么。
4. 推理：`AddDebugIntrinsics` 的 `optionalPrerequisiteOf = [Convert, AddDedupGroupAnnotations]` 说明了它相对这两个阶段的什么顺序？

**需要观察的现象**：不同阶段用不同的依赖维度表达需求。`prerequisites` 是硬性的，其余是柔性的顺序建议。

**预期结果**：你应该能口述——`CIRCT` 硬依赖 `circt.AddImplicitOutputFile`；`AddDebugIntrinsics` 硬依赖 `Elaborate`，并柔性地要求排在 `Convert`、`AddDedupGroupAnnotations` 之前；`AddDedupGroupAnnotations` 柔性地要求排在 `Convert` 之后（`optionalPrerequisites = [Convert]`）。

#### 4.2.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：`prerequisites` 与 `optionalPrerequisites` 的关键区别是什么？

**参考答案**：`prerequisites` 是**硬依赖**——声明的阶段一定会被拉进执行图并排在本阶段之前；`optionalPrerequisites` 是**软依赖**——仅当那个阶段**已经是**图中节点（被别人拉进来了）时，才要求它排在本阶段之前，否则不会因此把那个阶段拉进图。

**练习 2**：为什么几乎所有 Chisel `Phase` 都把 `invalidates` 重写成 `false`？

**参考答案**：这些阶段都是在往 `AnnotationSeq` 里**追加**注解（只增不改不删），彼此不破坏对方的产物。若保留默认值 `true`，则一个阶段跑完会让其他阶段「失效」，下游若还需要这些阶段就得重跑，造成无谓的重复劳动。设为 `false` 表示「我跑完不影响别人已建立的成果」。
